conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
Great! So, you had a pretty uneventful commute. What did you get up to this week?
Just the usual stuff, work and all that. Been trying to keep my head above water at the dental hygiene clinic where I work.
That sounds like a lot of responsibility. How's your workload been lately?
It's been tough, to be honest. We're short-staffed and I'm having trouble keeping up with all the appointments.
I can imagine that would be stressful. Have you noticed any changes in how you interact with your colleagues or patients during this time?
Yeah, I mean...guilt's been eating away at me a lot lately. Like, I just feel like I'm not doing my job right and it's all because of these scheduling issues.
It sounds like you're putting a lot of pressure on yourself to perform well. Can you tell me more about what you mean by "eating away at me"?
Yeah, it's like...I just feel awful about all the missed appointments and patients I've had to reschedule. And my boss is breathing down my neck.
That can be really tough. How do you think this guilt is affecting your daily life?
Honestly? It's hard for me to enjoy anything outside of work anymore. Even my usual hobbies, like reading or watching movies...it all just feels pointless right now.
I see. So, it sounds like this guilt is really impacting your sense of pleasure and enjoyment. Have you noticed any changes in how you're relating to others?
Yeah, my social life's suffered too. I've been withdrawing from friends and family because I just don't want to deal with their questions or worries about what's going on at work.
That makes sense. It can be hard to open up when you're feeling guilty or overwhelmed. What do you think would help you feel more connected to others right now?
Um, I'm not sure...I guess just knowing that they understand, you know? Like, if someone could relate to how crappy I'm feeling...
That makes sense. It can be really helpful to have someone who gets it. Have you noticed any other changes in your life recently?
Yeah, my interest in even doing things has pretty much vanished. I mean, I used to love going for walks during my breaks...now I just don't care.
That sounds like a big change. How do you think that's related to the guilt you're feeling?
Honestly? I think it's all tied together. Like, if I'm not doing well at work or my personal life is a mess, what's the point of even trying?
I see. So, it sounds like this sense of purpose and fulfillment has been disrupted for you. Have you thought about how you'd like to get your motivation back on track?
Yeah...I don't know, maybe just having someone to talk to about all this would help? Like, getting things off my chest...
Absolutely. Sometimes just sharing what's going on can be a huge relief. Let's keep exploring that.
Okay, sounds good to me.
So, how do you think we could work together to get your motivation and purpose back on track?
Um...I guess I'm open to suggestions? Maybe some kind of plan or something?
That's a great starting point. Let's start by identifying what small steps you can take towards getting your life back on track.
Yeah, okay...like what, exactly?
Well, what are some things that give you pleasure and enjoyment? Maybe we could build on those?
Hm...I used to love reading, I guess. And going for walks like I said before...
Those sound like great starting points! Let's make a plan to incorporate those into your daily routine.
Okay, sounds doable.
Great! What else can we work on to help you get started?
Um...I guess just having someone to check in with regularly? Like, accountability or something?
Absolutely. We can schedule regular sessions and I'll be here to support you as you work towards your goals.
Sounds good to me. Thanks for listening...
Of course, it's all part of the process!
